name: Salesforce, Inc.

profile.description: Salesforce, Inc. is the world's third-largest enterprise software company and largest enterprise applications company, with $41.5 billion in fiscal 2026 revenue (year ending January 2026). Founded by Marc Benioff, the company pioneered cloud-based CRM and is now leading the Agentic AI revolution with Agentforce, which achieved $800 million ARR in its first 15 months with 29,000+ deals closed. Salesforce targets $60 billion+ in organic revenue by FY2030.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Innovation Track Record (3/3): Salesforce launched Agentforce (agentic AI platform), achieving $800M ARR in 15 months with 29,000+ deals and 169% YoY ARR growth [1]. The company pioneered cloud CRM, expanded to a full enterprise platform (Sales, Service, Marketing, Commerce, Data Cloud), and is now defining the agentic AI category. AI Deployment (2/2): 11.14 trillion tokens served in Q4 FY2026, Agentforce and Data Cloud ARR of $1.8B combined, with 60%+ of bookings from existing customer expansions — demonstrating production-scale AI deployment with measurable revenue [1]. Market Share Leadership (2/2): Salesforce is the #1 CRM platform globally and the largest enterprise applications company, with $72B in total remaining performance obligations (+14% YoY) and 95% subscription revenue [1]. Product Pipeline (2/2): FY2027 guidance of $45.8-46.2B (10-11% growth), organic reacceleration in H2 FY2027, and $60B+ revenue target by FY2030 provide clear multi-year visibility [1]. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D spending is at industry average for enterprise software at scale.

inspiration.label: STRONG
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (4/4): Salesforce's five core values — trust, customer success, innovation, equality, and sustainability — have been validated by 25+ years of consistent execution [2]. Benioff's stakeholder capitalism model and famous statement 'business is the greatest platform for change' IS the competitive advantage, shaping everything from product decisions to M&A. Community Impact (2/2): The 1-1-1 model (1% equity, product, time) has been adopted by 20,000+ companies; Benioff personally donated $250M+ to UCSF Benioff Children's Hospitals and $150M to Hawaiian medical centers; co-founded 1t.org (trillion trees) [2]. Employee Retention (2/2): Salesforce consistently ranks among the best places to work globally, with employees citing strong sense of purpose and belonging [2]. Benioff's public advocacy for equal pay and workplace equality drives retention. Insider Buying (1/2): CEO Benioff holds significant equity as co-founder; the $50B share repurchase authorization signals institutional confidence, but specific insider buying data was not identified.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Salesforce's infrastructure is strong: forward P/E of 12.3 is attractive for a high-growth enterprise software company, profit margin of 18.0% approaches the 20% target, and ROE of 12.4% is solid [1]. Revenue grew 12.1% YoY to $41.5B with 95% subscription revenue. Non-GAAP operating margin of 34.1% demonstrates exceptional profitability. D/E of 29.9% is conservative. The $72B total RPO provides exceptional forward visibility. However, the stock has declined 26% in 2026 despite strong results, and muted FY2027 guidance (10-11% growth) concerned investors [1]. Liquidity is exceptional at $2.26B daily dollar volume.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (9/9): Salesforce IS AI infrastructure for enterprise software — Agentforce defines the agentic AI category with 29,000+ deals and $800M ARR [1]. The company's CRM data moat (customer data, interaction history, business processes across 150,000+ enterprise customers) cannot be replicated by external AI tools. Switching costs are massive — enterprises have built their entire customer operations on Salesforce. Rather than being disrupted by AI, Salesforce is the vehicle through which enterprises deploy AI agents. AI Opportunity (1/1): $1.8B combined Agentforce and Data Cloud ARR, 11.14 trillion tokens served in Q4, and 169% YoY ARR growth demonstrate massive, quantifiable AI revenue [1].

infinite_game.summary: Salesforce exemplifies infinite game thinking under founder Marc Benioff. The 1-1-1 philanthropic model adopted by 20,000+ companies, the stakeholder capitalism philosophy, and consistent values-driven leadership over 25+ years demonstrate genuine infinite game play. Benioff's willingness to invest in Agentforce during market skepticism and the $50B buyback show conviction in long-term value creation.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause is the competitive advantage (3/3). Benioff's vision of 'business as the greatest platform for change' has been validated by 25+ years of consistent execution [2]. The five core values (trust, customer success, innovation, equality, sustainability) shape every strategic decision — from the 1-1-1 model to the Agentforce investment. The cause survived multiple market cycles, leadership evolution, and industry disruptions, making it a genuine Just Cause by Sinek's definition.

trusting_teams.analysis: High-trust organization (2/2). Salesforce consistently ranks among the best places to work globally [2]. Benioff's public advocacy for equal pay (conducting company-wide pay equity audits), workplace equality, and employee empowerment creates demonstrated psychological safety. The 70,000-person workforce cites purpose and belonging as key motivators.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Infinite competitive mindset (1/1). Benioff noted that 'the vast majority of companies have mostly failed with their AI initiatives' and positioned Agentforce as a solution to this industry-wide problem [2]. Rather than attacking Microsoft (Copilot) or ServiceNow, Benioff frames the competitive landscape as an opportunity to help enterprises succeed with AI, using rivalry as motivation for improvement.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Some flexibility (1/2). Salesforce evolved from CRM to a comprehensive enterprise platform (Sales, Service, Marketing, Commerce) and is now pivoting to agentic AI with Agentforce [1]. Each expansion built on the CRM foundation rather than abandoning it. The company hasn't faced a truly existential pivot — the core SaaS subscription model remains unchanged through all expansions.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Some courageous decisions (1/2). Investing aggressively in Agentforce during market skepticism (stock down 26% in 2026) demonstrates conviction [1]. The $50B buyback authorization signals confidence. Benioff's public advocacy on climate, equality, and homelessness often put him at odds with shareholder expectations. However, the company hasn't made a major sacrifice of short-term earnings for long-term capability — AI investment has been margin-accretive.

investment_thesis: Salesforce is leading the enterprise AI revolution with Agentforce, which achieved $800M ARR in just 15 months with 29,000+ deals and 169% YoY growth — validating that enterprises are buying, not building, AI agents [1]. At 12.3x forward P/E (down 26% in 2026), the stock trades at a significant discount to its operational quality: $41.5B revenue growing 12.1%, 34.1% non-GAAP operating margin, 95% subscription revenue, and $72B total RPO [1]. Agentforce and Data Cloud ARR of $1.8B combined is accelerating ($1.4B just three months prior), and 75%+ of top 100 deals included both products. CEO Benioff's 25-year track record of values-driven innovation and the 1-1-1 philanthropic model distinguish Salesforce as a rare combination of financial performance and societal impact [2]. The $60B+ organic revenue target by FY2030 provides a clear long-term growth framework.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on decelerating growth: FY2027 guidance of $45.8-46.2B (10-11%) disappointed investors expecting AI-driven reacceleration, and the stock is down 26% in 2026 [1]. AI could ultimately disrupt the traditional seat-based SaaS model that drives 95% of Salesforce's revenue, as consumption-based AI pricing cannibalizes higher-margin per-seat subscriptions. Benioff himself noted that 'the vast majority of companies have mostly failed with their AI initiatives,' which applies to Salesforce's own customers' willingness to adopt Agentforce at scale [2]. The $50B buyback may be an implicit acknowledgment that organic growth opportunities are limited. Competition from Microsoft Copilot, ServiceNow, and emerging AI-native enterprise platforms is intensifying. At 23.4x trailing P/E, the stock is not cheap on current earnings despite the forward multiple looking attractive.
key_catalysts: Agentforce ARR accelerating from $800M to potentially $2B+ in FY2027, with 169% YoY growth and 22,000+ deals in Q4 alone [1]
H2 FY2027 organic revenue reacceleration as Agentforce and Data Cloud adoption broadens beyond early adopters [1]
$50B share repurchase authorization providing floor support while AI transformation matures [1]
key_risks: AI disruption of seat-based SaaS pricing model: consumption-based Agentforce pricing may cannibalize higher-margin per-seat subscriptions over time [1]
FY2027 guidance of 10-11% growth disappointed market expectations; if Agentforce growth decelerates, the narrative shifts from AI leader to mature software [1]
Microsoft Copilot and ServiceNow competing aggressively in enterprise AI; market share could fragment as more vendors offer agentic AI capabilities [2]
sources: Salesforce FY2026 Q4 and Full-Year Earnings Release, FY2027 Guidance, and Agentforce metrics, February 2026
Marc Benioff interviews — Cloud Wars, Forbes, SaaStr, and stakeholder capitalism advocacy, 2025-2026
